dialogos.texto_en: (A few days have passed since\n returning from the graduation trip.\n The day to head to Tokyo had finally come.)
(Before heading to the station,\n I stopped by the quintuplets' apartment to greet Miku,\n and for another reason%p-2;――%p;)
(There are so many memories here.\n Seriously, so many things happened.)
What's wrong? Fidgeting around like that.
Yeah...\nI can't help from feeling emotional\nthinking that I won't be coming here for a while.
That's right...\nI wonder when we will be able to meet again?
I can't say for sure,\nbut I do plan on coming back during long holidays.
Yeah...
Sorry for making you come all the way here.\nWill you make it to your train on time?
Don't worry, I came with plenty of time to spare.
So, what is it that you wanted to give me?
Oh, right.\nI wanted you to take this with you.
...A letter set?
Yes, Ryukyu washi paper stationery and envelopes.\nI found them during the graduation trip\nand bought them for you.
I want you to use them to write letters to me\nonce you've settled into life in Tokyo.
Write letters...
Of course, I'll write some too.
So, we'll be pen pals?
Yes, pen pals.\n...Is that okay?
That's fine.\nLet's be pen pals.\nI like how it's kind of old-fashioned, in a way%p-2;――
No, I get it.\nIn a time where we have email and social media,\nwriting letters can be refreshing experience.
Yeah. I haven't written a letter in a long time either.\nI think I've only sent New Year's cards.
But, why?
During the graduation trip,\nwe couldn't express our feelings to each other\nand things got awkward.
At that time, it was very difficult to establish a\nproper dialogue.\nBut that's when I realized the importance of it.
That's why I thought it would be nice\nto communicate our feelings to each other through writing.
I want to express my feelings word for word and send them\nto you, as well as I'd like to receive them from you.
And also%p-2;――
During the time we're apart,\nI want to hold onto the words\nthat we've shared with each other.
...While we're apart.
I understand. I'll write down everything I saw\nand felt that day and send it all to you, Miku.
Okay. That's what I want.
Yeah. Not because it's anything special,\nbut just things we want to express. I'll do the same.
Yeah.
But hey, just because you can't write kanji,\ndon't go making them on the computer.\nIt'll lose all its sentiment.
Mmmh...\nYou're making fun of me too much, Futaro.\nI was able to graduate, so I can write some kanji.
Haha, yeah, you're right.\nI know the best out of anyone how much you've grown as a person.
Okay, then I'll send my letter first.\nLooking forward to it.
Yeah, I'll be expecting.
%p-2;――%p; Now then, it's about time.
Ah...
(Seeing Miku's lonely expression,\n I hesitate to leave.\n But%p-2;――%p;)
Futaro.\nDo your best in Tokyo.
Yeah. You too, good luck at your vocational school.
(I have a strong bond with Miku.\n Without a doubt.)
Well then, I'm off.
Have a safe trip, Futaro.
